title: papaver rhoeas subsp. intermedium
intro: Papaver rhoeas subsp. intermedium, also known as the Shirley poppy or intermediate poppy, is a subspecies of Papaver rhoeas, a flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, due to the presence of alkaloids such as protopine and sanguinarine. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas subsp. intermedium include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Papaver rhoeas subsp. intermedium include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
